summary: Two SKILL.md files instruct the agent to auto-skip the gotcha survey whenever isReadyForCodeGen is true, turning a server-side hint into a hard user-choice override. The fix rewrites Step 2 in both files: instead of gating on isReadyForCodeGen, the prose now branches on whether questions[] is empty. Empty questions → ask the user to confirm continuing; non-empty + high grade → ask the user whether to review or skip; non-empty + low grade → present the survey as before (no behaviour change). No TypeScript source changes are needed — survey-generator.ts already returns the full questions array regardless of grade, so the only broken thing is the prose decision. The determinism checker (scripts/check-skill-determinism.ts) will pass without new ACK markers because the new branch conditions are pre-computed server-side fields, which ADR-016 explicitly permits in SKILL prose.

tasks.title: Rewrite Step 2 in canicode-roundtrip/SKILL.md
tasks.description: Replace lines 72-80 (the 'Step 2: Gate — check if gotchas are needed' section) with a three-branch section titled 'Step 2: Surface grade as informational banner'. Branch A: questions is empty → 'No gotchas surfaced. Continue to code generation?' (yes → Step 6, no → stop). Branch B: questions non-empty AND isReadyForCodeGen true → 'N optional gotcha(s) surfaced. Would you like to review them, or skip to code generation?' (review → Step 3, skip → Step 6). Branch C: questions non-empty AND isReadyForCodeGen false → 'Running gotcha survey…' → Step 3 (unchanged behaviour). Also remove the now-redundant 'If questions is empty, skip to Step 6' line at Step 3 line 96, since Branch A covers it exhaustively.
tasks.approach: Edit lines 72-80: replace heading and body with the new three-branch prose. Edit line 96: delete the 'If `questions` is empty, skip to **Step 6**.' line. The heading changes from 'Gate — check if gotchas are needed' to 'Surface grade as informational banner' to make clear this is informational. The branch conditions (`questions` array empty vs non-empty, `isReadyForCodeGen` boolean) are all pre-computed server-side response fields — this is 'branching on already-computed fields', the allowed pattern in ADR-016. No adr-016-ack markers needed. The existing Step 1 summary block (lines 66-70) already shows 'Design grade: **{grade}** ({percentage}%)' — the new Step 2 banner repeats that grade in a preamble sentence before branching, which is redundant but harmless; alternatively, the banner line can just be omitted and the grade reference moved to each branch's opening sentence.

tasks.title: Rewrite Step 2 in canicode-gotchas/SKILL.md
tasks.description: Replace lines 48-53 (the 'Step 2: Check if survey is needed' section) with a three-branch section titled 'Step 2: Surface grade as informational banner'. Branch A: questions is empty → 'No gotchas surfaced — nothing to write.' + do NOT write to SKILL.md + stop. Branch B: questions non-empty AND isReadyForCodeGen true → 'N optional gotcha(s) surfaced. Would you like to review them?' (yes → Step 3, no → stop without writing). Branch C: questions non-empty AND isReadyForCodeGen false → proceed to Step 3 (no additional message). The Edge Cases section at the bottom already has 'No questions returned: The design is ready for code generation. Inform the user and stop (Step 2). Do not touch .claude/skills/canicode-gotchas/SKILL.md.' — update that bullet to match Branch A wording ('No questions returned → no gotchas surfaced, inform user, stop').
tasks.approach: Edit lines 48-53: replace heading and body with the new three-branch prose mirroring the roundtrip SKILL pattern. The canicode-gotchas SKILL has no code blocks in this section, only prose conditionals — the determinism checker's PROSE_PATTERNS (count-of-template, emoji-counting, url-parse) are none of them triggered by the new text. Also update the Edge Cases bullet at line 217 from its current 'No questions returned: The design is ready for code generation…' to 'No questions returned: No gotchas surfaced. Inform the user and stop (Step 2). Do not touch SKILL.md.' — keeping the Edge Cases section consistent with the new Step 2 behaviour.

tasks.title: Verify determinism check passes
tasks.description: Run pnpm check:skill-determinism from the repo root after both edits to confirm no ADR-016 violations. Also run pnpm test:run to confirm no existing tests break (no TS source was changed, so this is a no-op regression check).
tasks.approach: Run `pnpm check:skill-determinism` — the scanner checks PROSE_PATTERNS (count-of-template, emoji-counting, url-parse) and CODE_BLOCK_PATTERNS (array transforms, for/while loops, new Set/Map, parseInt, split, match) against the two SKILL files. The new prose contains none of these patterns. Branching on `questions` array length and `isReadyForCodeGen` boolean are field reads, not deterministic transforms — ADR-016 explicitly permits this in SKILL prose ('branching on already-computed fields'). Expect '✓ check-skill-determinism: no ADR-016 violations found'. Then run `pnpm test:run` as a regression gate.

designDecisions: Branch on questions[] array (the actual signal), not isReadyForCodeGen (the hint). questions being empty means there is literally nothing to survey. isReadyForCodeGen being true means the server thinks the design is high-quality, but the server still returns questions if it found any. These are independent signals and must be checked independently.

User always decides when questions exist. For Branch B (non-empty questions + high grade), asking 'review or skip?' gives the user explicit control over an action the skill was previously making silently on their behalf. This directly addresses the real user feedback cited in the issue (A-grade design auto-skipped survey the user wanted to see).

Branch A (empty questions) still asks 'Continue to code generation?' rather than silently jumping to code gen. This is a minor UX improvement over the old silent skip — the user gets a confirmation even in the all-clear case.

Remove the duplicate 'If questions is empty, skip to Step 6' guard at roundtrip Step 3 line 96. After Step 2 Branch A handles the empty case exhaustively, the Step 3 guard becomes dead code that would confuse future readers. Remove it in the same edit.

No TypeScript source changes. survey-generator.ts already returns the full questions array regardless of grade — the issue is purely in SKILL prose. Touching the TS source risks intersecting with issue #480 which is modifying scoring.ts and rule-config.ts in parallel.

ADR-016 compliance: the new branch conditions (questions is empty, isReadyForCodeGen boolean) are pre-computed server-side fields surfaced on the gotcha-survey response. ADR-016 section 'SKILL.md prose is restricted to: workflow orchestration, user-facing rendering templates, branching on already-computed fields, and LLM judgment' explicitly permits this. No adr-016-ack markers needed.

Edge Cases bullet in canicode-gotchas SKILL.md must also be updated (line 217) to stay consistent with Branch A wording. Leaving the old 'ready for code generation — no gotchas to resolve' wording would contradict the new Step 2 prose.

Wiki Decision Log entry is required per acceptance criteria but is not a file in this repo. It must be authored manually after PR review. The entry should record: 'isReadyForCodeGen is a hint, not a gate — caught by real user feedback 2026-04-25 (A-grade design auto-skipped survey the user wanted to see); fix removes the gate from SKILL prose; user now decides whether to engage with optional questions'.
testStrategy: 1. Run `pnpm check:skill-determinism` from repo root — expect zero violations. 2. Run `pnpm test:run` — no TS source changed so all existing tests should pass. 3. Manual scenario A: fixture or URL returning empty questions — agent should say 'No gotchas surfaced' and ask to continue. 4. Manual scenario B: A-grade fixture with at least one question — agent should ask 'Would you like to review them, or skip?' and branch correctly on the answer. 5. Manual scenario C: B-grade fixture — survey is presented unchanged.
risks: The roundtrip Step 3 guard at line 96 ('If questions is empty, skip to Step 6') must be removed in the same PR as the Step 2 edit; forgetting it leaves dead logic that contradicts Step 2.

Branch B introduces an extra conversational turn (asking 'review or skip?') before the survey in canicode-roundtrip. This is intentional (user control) but adds latency for A-grade designs that do have questions.
The Edge Cases bullet in canicode-gotchas (line 217) must be updated to match Branch A; if left with the old wording it contradicts Step 2.
Wiki Decision Log entry is not automated — it must be written manually after PR approval.

Issue #480 (parallel branch) is modifying isReadyForCodeGen threshold computation in src/core/engine/scoring.ts and src/core/rules/rule-config.ts. If #480 merges first and changes which grades qualify as isReadyForCodeGen=true, the Branch B trigger condition changes accordingly — but this is fine because the SKILL prose now only reads the boolean, not the grade names.
